examples.title: Pseudocode: AWT and Swing Hierarchy
examples.description: Demonstrating the hierarchical relationship between AWT and Swing components
examples.code:
CREATE top-level container (JFrame)
CREATE container (JPanel)
CREATE AWT Button
CREATE Swing JButton
ADD AWT Button to container
ADD Swing JButton to container
ADD container to top-level container
DISPLAY top-level container
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the hierarchical relationship between AWT and Swing components. It creates a top-level container (JFrame), a container (JPanel), an AWT Button, and a Swing JButton. The AWT Button and Swing JButton are added to the container, which is then added to the top-level container.

examples.title: Java: AWT and Swing Hierarchy
examples.description: Demonstrating the hierarchical relationship between AWT and Swing components
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the hierarchical relationship between AWT and Swing components. It creates a JFrame (Swing's top-level container), a JPanel (Swing's container), an AWT Button, and a Swing JButton. The AWT Button and Swing JButton are added to the JPanel, which is then added to the JFrame.

examples.title: Java: Event Model
examples.description: Demonstrating the event model used by AWT and Swing
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the event model used by AWT and Swing. It creates a JFrame, a JButton, and adds an ActionListener to the button. When the button is clicked, the ActionListener's actionPerformed method is called, printing "Button clicked" to the console.
